folio.label: 104r
folio.l: Be now nomore a gast , ne euele apayd
I haue thy feitú , and thyn benygnytee
As wel as euäe womman , was assayed
In gret a stat , and poorely arrayed
Now knowe I deere wyf , thy stedfastnesse
And hire in armes toke , and gan hire kysse
¢ And she for wonder , toke of it no kepe
She herde nat , what thyngö he to hire seyde
She ferd as she had stirt , out of a slepe
Tyl she , out of hire Masednesse , a breyde
Grisilde q¶ he , by god that for vs deyde
Thow art my wyf , noon other I haue
Ne neuäe had , as god my soule saue
¢ This is thy dougúter , whicú æè hast supposed
To be my wyf , that other feitúfully
Shal be myn Eyr , as I haue ay disposed
Thow bar hi~ , in thy body trewely
At Boloigne , haue I kept hi~ preuyly
Take he~ a geyn , for now maist æè nat sey
That thow hast lorn~ , noone of æine children twey
¢ And folk that other wyse , han seyd on me
I warne he~ wele , that I haue don this dede
For no malice , ne for no cruelte
But for to assaye , in the , thy wommanhede
And nat to slen myne children , I I god forbede
But for to kepen he~ , preuyly and stille
Til I thy purpos knew , and al thy wylle
¢ Whan she this herd , on swou©ne doun she falletú
For pytous ioye , and aftir hire swownyngö
She , bothe hire ©onge children , vn to hire calletú
And in hire armes , pytously wepyngö
Embracede hem , and tenderly kyssyngö
Ful lyke a Moder , witú hire salte teres
She bathed bothe here visage , and here heres
¢ O whicú a pytous thyngö , it was to se
Hire swownyngö , and hire humble voys to heere
Graunt mäcy lord , god thanke it ©ow q¶ she
That ©e han saued me , myne children dere
Now rekke I neuäe , to be ded rigút heere
Sithe I stonde , in ©oure loue , and in ©oure grace
No force of detú , ne whan my spirit pace
¢ O tendre o dere , o ©onge children myne
ñoure wooful Moder , wend stedfastly
That cruel houndes , or som foule venyme
Had eten ©ow , but god of his mäcy
